items.title: Enhance A1 SSG Plan for Instant First Paint in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a detailed implementation plan for the A1 workstream, focusing on server-side generation (SSG) for public pages. Key changes include a feature-flag safety model, allowing for controlled rollouts, and a phased approach to implementation. The plan aims to provide users with an instant, content-rich first paint while maintaining interactivity, significantly improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance homepage with real SLA uptime card in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update reintroduces the homepage uptime stat card, now powered by actual data from the `api_usage.json` snapshot instead of a static value. The card will only display when real uptime data is available, ensuring users see accurate information. The current uptime value is approximately 99.7% over the last 6 hours.

items.title: Enhanced SLA Metrics for API Usage in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a real, request-weighted SLA for the API usage snapshot, adding `sla.last_6h` and `sla.last_24h` metrics. These metrics provide accurate uptime calculations by excluding synthetic probe data and only counting 5xx errors as downtime. The changes ensure that the homepage will eventually reflect these real-time SLA values, enhancing transparency and reliability.

items.title: Revamped Multi-Modal Directory and Pricing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a multi-modal directory featuring various modalities including chat, image, video, music, and search, expanding the model count from 55 to 78. It also implements package-aware tier pricing, allowing for dynamic pricing based on usage tiers. The redesign enhances the user interface with a professional card-grid gallery and improved accessibility features.

items.title: Enhance Multi-Modal Model Catalog with Tiered Pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive multi-modal model catalog, featuring 78 items across five modalities, each with entry and best tier pricing. The new API endpoint allows users to filter models by modality and ensures accurate pricing based on real package tiers. Additionally, tests have been added to verify the multi-modality and tiered pricing functionality.

items.title: Refactor to Integrate @acedatacloud/core in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the first implementation of the shared @acedatacloud/core in the PlatformFrontend, aligning with the Nexior rollout. Key changes include the renaming of utility functions and the introduction of behavior-preserving shims, resulting in a reduction of 200 lines of code. The integration enhances functionality while maintaining existing interfaces, ensuring a smooth transition for developers.

items.title: New Models and Pricing Directory Launch in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new, SEO-friendly directory for models and pricing, accessible via the `/models` and `/pricing` endpoints. This feature includes a searchable directory with real-time pricing comparisons and savings badges, enhancing user experience and transparency. The implementation is flag-gated, ensuring a safe rollout while supporting multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance chunk-load guard for entry signatures in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update broadens the chunk-load entry-signature regex to accommodate both `index-<hash>.js` and `index.<hash>.js` formats. This change ensures that the stale-chunk reload guard functions correctly for the PlatformFrontend's dotted entry. The update is backward-compatible with Nexior on versions `^0.2.0`, and includes 22 new tests that have all passed.

items.title: Launch Public Pricing API and Sitemap for Models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a public per-model pricing API, allowing users to access pricing details for each model via the new endpoint `/api/v1/models/?with_pricing=1`. The pricing is dynamically calculated based on a single source of truth, ensuring accuracy and consistency with billing. Additionally, a sitemap and robots.txt have been added to enhance discoverability for the `/models` and `/pricing` directories.

items.title: Fix prerender for HTTPS URLs to enhance bot rendering in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This hotfix addresses an issue where bots received an empty HTML response due to incorrect URL handling in the prerender setup. The rewrite rule has been updated to ensure that HTTPS URLs are always used, allowing bots to receive the correct rendered content. Verification shows that the changes successfully render the expected JSON-LD output without affecting human users.

items.title: Pricing adjustments for video services in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update implements significant price reductions for grok-video and midjourney-video services based on a recent profitability audit. Key changes include lowering prices to align more closely with competitor rates while maintaining healthy margins. The adjustments are expected to enhance competitiveness and attract more users to these services.

items.title: Deprecate Sora APIs as Service Goes Offline in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update marks the Sora APIs as deprecated following the service being taken offline due to consistent failures. The two API routes, `/sora/videos` and `/sora/tasks`, have been removed from the documentation and will now return a 404 error. The changes are reversible, allowing for potential restoration in the future.

items.title: Revamped Slack Marketplace Tracker in Index 🚀
items.summary: The Slack Marketplace plan documentation has been refreshed to serve as a live follow-up tracker, eliminating the outdated duplicate. Key updates include a redefined app status, trimmed OAuth scopes, and completion of all submission steps, although a blocker remains due to the requirement of at least five active workspaces for app review submission.

items.title: Enhance Crawlable Models & Pricing Directory in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a detailed plan for a crawlable `/models` and `/pricing` directory, enhancing discoverability as outlined in the INFLUENCE-AND-DISCOVERABILITY strategy. It leverages the existing public API and integrates per-model pricing, ensuring a single source of truth. The new features will be deployed behind a feature flag, allowing for controlled rollout and verification across multiple build PRs.

items.title: Transition to Self-Hosted Prerender for Enhanced Crawler Visibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully migrates the frontend to a self-hosted prerender solution, replacing the previous reliance on prerender.io. Key changes include the update of the PRERENDER_URL to point to the in-cluster service and the removal of the unused PRERENDER_TOKEN. This transition ensures that bots receive fully-rendered HTML, improving visibility and performance without incurring additional costs.

items.title: Final Core Adoption for HTTP Client in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update finalizes the integration of the core HTTP client for Nexior, replacing the previous axios instance with `createHttpClient` from `@acedatacloud/core`. Key features such as request headers and error handling remain consistent, ensuring seamless functionality. The change includes extensive testing coverage and has been validated with linting and build checks.

items.title: Clean up ACE extension link in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the redundant `?category=development` from the ACE extension guide link in the BYOC credentials dialog. The link now directly points to the document, leveraging tag-derivation for the sidebar category. This change is cosmetic, as the old links remain functional.

items.title: Enhance Slack Integration with New Meta Tag in Nexior 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a new meta tag for Slack App Suggestions by adding `<meta name="slack-app-id" content="A0B0VU3KBQX" />` to the site's `<head>`. This change will help surface our Slack app as a suggestion when links from this domain are shared in Slack workspaces. The addition does not alter existing behavior and the App ID is public.

items.title: Streamline chunk-load recovery with @acedatacloud/core in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the chunk-load error handling in Nexior by integrating the createChunkLoadErrorHandler from the @acedatacloud/core package. The existing functionality remains unchanged, ensuring seamless operation while reducing the codebase by 90 lines. This marks the first implementation of the shared core package within Nexior, with successful builds confirmed using vue-tsc and vite.

items.title: Streamline Telemetry and Fingerprint Integration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the telemetry and fingerprint functionalities in Nexior by integrating two core modules from @acedatacloud/core. The telemetry now utilizes `createTelemetry`, significantly reducing code size, while the fingerprinting process leverages `createFingerprintResolver` with lazy loading to optimize performance. Additionally, the dependency on @acedatacloud/core has been updated to version 0.2.0.

items.title: Streamline Document URLs and Sidebar Categories in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the documentation page by deriving sidebar categories directly from document tags, eliminating the need for `?category=` in URLs. A new utility has been introduced to manage category mapping, ensuring clean and shareable links while maintaining existing functionality for legacy URLs. The implementation also improves user experience by preventing sidebar flashes on cold loads.

items.title: Enhance Type Checking with typesVersions in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a `typesVersions` map for various subpaths in the core package, addressing type-checking issues for apps using `moduleResolution: "node"`. The version has been bumped from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0, ensuring compatibility with TypeScript 5.8. All changes have been verified, and the runtime remains stable with Vite.

items.title: Pricing & Margin Audit Insights in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive pricing and margin audit for 34 services and 254 SKUs, revealing that while we are cheaper than competitors on approximately 120 SKUs, nearly 48% of our offerings are priced below upstream costs. Key issues identified include credit-value de-anchoring and placeholder pricing strategies. The report outlines a remediation roadmap aimed at addressing these challenges and optimizing our pricing strategy.

items.title: Introducing a Self-Hosted Prerender Service in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a self-hosted headless-Chromium prerender service to replace the paid prerender.io, which was returning 503 errors. The new service is additive and does not alter the existing PRERENDER_URL, ensuring current functionality remains intact. Future updates will switch the URL to the new service after successful deployment verification.

items.title: Updated Plan Statuses and Archived Shipped Plans in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the statuses of active plans by verifying them against merged PRs and live data, resulting in the archiving of 8 plans that have already been shipped. The README has been rewritten to reflect these changes and now includes new plans from parallel work. Additionally, a critical security issue has been identified that requires immediate attention.

items.title: Package renamed to @acedatacloud/core in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the package from `@acedatacloud/common-frontend` to `@acedatacloud/core`, streamlining the scope name. Key files such as package.json, README, and the release workflow have been updated accordingly. There are no behavioral changes, and all tests are passing successfully.

items.title: Enhance Homepage Stats and Prerender Timeout in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes hardcoded fabricated statistics from the homepage, ensuring that metrics are only displayed when backed by real data. Additionally, the prerender timeout for bot user agents has been increased from 10 seconds to 30 seconds, improving the crawlability of heavy pages. These changes aim to enhance trust and discoverability for users.

items.title: Enhance Influence & Discoverability Strategy in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive analysis and phased execution plan aimed at addressing the influence and discoverability challenges faced by AceDataCloud. Key changes include the addition of a new document detailing the current gaps and a structured plan to enhance crawlability, content visibility, and community trust. Immediate actions focus on fixing broken elements and eliminating misleading statistics to improve overall platform reach.

items.title: Transform CommonFrontend into NPM Package in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update pivots CommonFrontend to a published NPM package, `@acedatacloud/common-frontend`, consolidating shared infrastructure across multiple applications. Key features include a config-driven architecture for HTTP clients, telemetry, fingerprinting, and locale management, all built with TypeScript and designed for optimal tree-shaking. The CI/CD workflows are set up for automated testing and publishing, enhancing the development process.

items.title: Streamlined Planning Docs in Index 📚
items.summary: This update consolidates all planning documents into a single, organized `plans/` directory, enhancing navigability and clarity. Key changes include the deletion of 5 outdated documents, archiving of 8 reference docs, and the grouping of approximately 20 active plans into themed folders. Legal artifacts have also been moved to a dedicated `_legal/` folder, ensuring better compliance tracking.

items.title: Unifying Claude.ai MCP Guides for Flux and Sora in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the documentation for the Flux and Sora MCP guides under the correct naming convention, ensuring they are properly nested within the Claude.ai/Desktop MCP series. Key changes include renaming files, updating cross-links across 93 files, and correcting titles to match the series. This enhances the organization and accessibility of the documentation for users.

items.title: Enhance Slack Integration with New Meta Tag in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `<meta>` tag for the Slack app ID in the site's `<head>`, enabling Slack to suggest our app when links are shared. The addition of this tag does not alter existing behaviors but enhances visibility in the Slack Marketplace. The app ID is public and serves as a key component of our distribution setup.

items.title: Streamline Skill Management by Moving Marketing Video Factory in Skills 🎥
items.summary: The marketing-video-factory skill has been successfully removed from the customer Skills package and relocated to PlatformStudio. This change aligns the orchestration playbook with its implementation, reducing drift and enhancing clarity. The single-service skills it orchestrates remain unchanged in the customer package.

items.title: Streamline Marketing Video Production in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update moves the marketing-video-factory orchestration skill into PlatformStudio, ensuring it is closely aligned with its implementation. Key additions include a comprehensive playbook and shared documentation for authentication and async tasks, enhancing usability and self-containment. The single-service skills remain unchanged in the customer Skills package, maintaining a clear separation of concerns.

items.title: Streamlined Slack Scopes and Connector Insights in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update documents the successful trimming of Slack app scopes, reducing bot scopes from 72 to 15 and user scopes to zero. Key findings reveal that the app functions as a data connector rather than a traditional in-Slack bot, necessitating a reinstall for the new scopes to take effect. Additional details about privacy and terms have also been included in the documentation.

items.title: Enhance TTS Pricing Display in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the rendering of TTS pricing on the service pricing page by replacing the raw JSON display with a user-friendly format. The changes include unwrapping the minimum charge guard and displaying rates per 1M bytes instead of per byte. Additionally, it addresses similar issues for the Fish TTS API, ensuring consistency across the platform.

items.title: Retire openai-audio service and merge TTS pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The standalone openai-audio service has been retired and its API has been successfully integrated into the openai service. Key changes include the deletion of the openai-audio service and the addition of TTS pricing rules to the openai service's display pricing. This streamlines our services and ensures that TTS pricing is now clearly listed under the openai service.

items.title: Revamp Talking Photo i18n and Model Requirements in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update fixes several issues with the Talking Photo feature, including translation errors, model requirements, and layout inconsistencies. Key changes include the proper wrapping of i18n keys, ensuring the model is always sent during generation, and adding model and mode selectors for improved user experience. These enhancements ensure a smoother and more reliable functionality for users.
